notes: Eval 3 (topic-mismatch) passes 100% in both configurations — non-discriminating. Claude's base capability detects topic mismatches without skill guidance.
Eval 1 discriminating assertion: execution tracker with Gates is skill-specific (always fails without skill). Other 6/7 assertions pass in both configs.
Eval 2 discriminating assertion: protocol persistence to review/protocol.md. Without skill, the agent saves to outputs/ instead of the correct path.
Eval 1 thin-evidence warning assertion passes trivially in baseline (no warning mechanism exists).
With-skill runs are faster on average (91s vs 105s) despite more tool calls, suggesting more structured execution.
The skill's main added value is structural: execution tracker, correct file paths, gate-based progression. Domain knowledge (PICO, search strategy) is strong in baseline too.
